slides.eyebrow: Education · Publishing rights: what composers give up without knowing
slides.headline: A composer we spoke to in Month 2 had three confirmed placements.
But he had never registered publishing before the licences were signed.
slides.lede: Music already aired. Already used. Fees already paid.

slides.headline: Estimated performance royalties gone: over ₦800K. Uncollectable. Permanently.
Here's the publishing rights breakdown nobody teaches in Lagos. ↓
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: WHAT PUBLISHING RIGHTS ACTUALLY CONTROL: · Who collects your performance royalties every time the content airs · Who approves sync licence requests — if they can say no, they can block placements · Who shares in your sync fee (publishers take a cut — know the percentage)
list_items: Who collects your performance royalties every time the content airs
Who approves sync licence requests — if they can say no, they can block placements
Who shares in your sync fee (publishers take a cut — know the percentage)
How your music can be used after the deal is signed

slides.lede:
You sign for distribution.
The small print gives them 50% of your publishing income — including sync.

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: WHAT TO PROTECT: · Self-publish where possible: register directly with COSON, SAMRO, PRS · If signing a publishing deal: keep sync approval rights in your name · Document splits with co-writers before any deal reaches the table
list_items: Self-publish where possible: register directly with COSON, SAMRO, PRS
If signing a publishing deal: keep sync approval rights in your name
Document splits with co-writers before any deal reaches the table
Register every finished track before any placement conversation starts

slides.headline: One sync fee pays once.
slides.lede: Published royalties from a single placement can pay for years.

slides.headline: Don't leave the second half of the deal unregistered.
Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.lede: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
